## [00:00 - 03:02]

Have you ever heard of schema data and markup data? These are some of the hidden things behind how to make Facebook ads effective and any kind of advertising effective. And it all goes back to the days of SEO when websites were launched, but there was no context for search bots to understand what they're actually looking at in the code. Because when search bots look at your website, what they actually see is quite different to what you're actually looking at as a user. So it sees HTML. So in this case, if we look at the source of this website, we can see lots of code. Now, what happens with bots is that they are looking for some standardized data. So schema.org has structured data in a way that becomes uniform across many websites. So what Facebook also does is look for particular things when your products are set up. So for example, if we go back to here and we'll look at the Facebook pixel, we can see that on this product page, view content is the event that's being fired. And we can see that these are the data points being set by Shopify in this case. So we can see the value of the product. We can see the currency, you can see the category, content ID, and that gets passed back to the Facebook pixel. That is also available in the code. So the code uses schema.org. So if we do a search for, let's say price currency in our code here, which by the way, you don't need to understand it, but it's useful to know what's going on as well. So here, this is the same page and you can see the information is here. So it's been set up in a way that allows search engines and also the Facebook pixel to understand what's going on on the page and what's actually being presented. So here, for example, we have the price, which is just a set of numbers, but we add context by adding the currency. We say that this is a type of offer. So here, schema.org slash offer. And this page defines everything that you can put together against an offer. So search engines and bots and, you know, ads, Facebook ads, bot and everything like that can understand the context of what's on the page. Now, you might be wondering, I don't care for all the technical stuff. As long as it works, it's all fine. Why this matters is when this is set up and it's set up properly. And in most cases, when you're running Shopify, it's automatically done, so you don't need to worry. Is that before you've even run an ad, Facebook has this data. Facebook knows that you're running a product, that you have a price, you have a currency, and there's some information on the product as well. Why this is useful is,

## [03:02 - 04:54]

when, if you imagine running broad targeting, you've got no interest, no look likes, and nothing, anything like that, Facebook does have some data. It knows your price points. It knows your currency. In most cases, you know, when you've got a currency set, it knows the country that you're aiming for, but you may have that target as well. And it has some contextual information on who you're trying to target. This goes into the pixel data. It goes into your ad set targeting. It gets recorded all throughout. So this is why it's important to make sure that you have this installed. Now, if you're on Shopify, it should be done automatically. You can double check as I've just showed you, just search for price currency and make sure this is available in your HTML code. And you can check what data is available, especially if you wanna go a lot more advanced and you can enhance this data as well. So there's lots of opportunities. When you do enhance that data, you're giving more context. It's not just Facebook, it's any ad pixel that's installed in your site, and also Google as well, because have you ever seen, you do a search for a cinema listing and an organic search result comes back, it shows the time, it shows the location, maybe even the price of the ticket in Google's organic search results, that's done through schema.org. So if I go back to the different types of schemas available, there are many. There's restaurant, local business, et cetera, et cetera. This has been set up so that bots can understand the context of what's on your page. That helps your organic, your SEO, but it also helps the ads engine as well to understand the context of what's actually happening on your website. So just make sure, even if you don't understand the detail of it, that it is available and it is installed because it will help you in the long run.
